creator: raimeetravel
caption:
I wish I knew about these places in Tokyo on my first trip! This was the perfect mix of playful + cultural and reminded me why I love this city so much.

 • getting humbled at Taito arcade lol
 • watching sumo up close & fighting a sumo wrestler myself! (so cool)
 • Mandarake treasure hunting
 • ice cream deep in the underground mall

The perfect Tokyo chaos day!!! Comment BLOG & I’ll send you my full guide to this off the beaten path trip!

 #tokyooldmeetsnew @tokyotokyooldmeetsnew #pr

transcription.text: This is my not-so-obvious guide for one day in Tokyo, so if you want to get off the beaten path and do some more unique things, I got you. You'll start your day at Taito Station Asakusa. This is the best way to feel like a kid again. I did lose many times, but I also won a few times, too. Then just wander around this whole area. It's peak Tokyo energy. Next, you'll head over to the Asakusa Sumo Club. There's a live show. You'll get a free drink, plus all you can eat. They also explain the rules and demo the techniques, so it's a really cool way to just learn about what sumo is. And if you're lucky, you might even get to participate like I did. Yep, that's me. And head over to Mandarake. The biggest location is at the Nakano Broadway. It is heaven if you love manga, vintage toys, or just exploring very weird and cool collectibles. Another recommendation for this day, go grab ice cream from this place. They have so many incredible soft-serve flavors, and you can even stack them all on top of each other if you're feeling brave enough to not let it fall over and finish the whole thing. Then you'll end your day with an izakaya dinner at this spot. The design was inspired by the John Wick films. And here's your cozy hotel room you're going to be staying at. I've never seen any of these things recommended before, so save this for your next trip to Tokyo.
